# UX Checklist: Show Missing-Data Warnings in UI

**Purpose**: Validate warning display, confidence indicators, and user controls.
**Created**: 2026-03-11
**Feature**: `specs/026-missing-data-warnings/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/prompts:speckit.checklist` workflow.

## Warning Presentation

- [x] CHK-UX-001 Confidence indicator shows percentage and label when available.
- [x] CHK-UX-002 Standard partial-data warnings use banner-style panel.
- [x] CHK-UX-003 Missing factors are listed with impact explanations.
- [x] CHK-UX-004 Factor breakdown marks unavailable factors.

## Severity Variants

- [x] CHK-UX-005 Very low confidence shows prominent warning without hiding estimate.
- [x] CHK-UX-006 Minor missing factors show a small non-blocking notice.
- [x] CHK-UX-007 Routing fallback shows straight-line approximation message.

## Interaction

- [x] CHK-UX-008 Warning details are expandable without breaking layout.
- [x] CHK-UX-009 Dismissal collapses panel but leaves persistent indicator.
- [x] CHK-UX-010 Users can reopen warnings from the indicator.

## Consistency & Accessibility

- [x] CHK-UX-011 Labels align with shared naming conventions ("Confidence", "Missing data", "Approximation").
- [x] CHK-UX-012 Basic accessibility pass (keyboard navigation + contrast) is part of UX gate.
